beats.activity: Wake up, check rooftop garden, quick coffee and toast
beats.reasoning: Starting the day at home in Northgate after yesterday's low energy (35/100). The heat stress from yesterday is gone, and cooler weather (68F) means Mateo will likely feel more energized. Garden check is part of his morning ritual.

beats.activity: Bike ride to Georgetown shop, avoiding transit strike
beats.reasoning: Metro strike until 4pm means all buses and Link are down. Mateo is a bike mechanic who lives by his bike—cycling from Northgate to Georgetown is his natural move. ~6 km ride, ~50 min in mild conditions. Arrives ready for work.

beats.activity: Work: tune-ups, repairs, and weekend route prep at the shop
beats.reasoning: Steady morning and early afternoon at the shop. Cooler weather means fewer heat-related customer complaints. Workaholic tendency keeps him focused on repairs and planning weekend cycling guide routes.
Comfortable routine meal spot. Mateo knows this place well from recent days, it's close to the shop, and pho is his reliable refuel during work.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: consultations, drivetrain upgrades, route planning
beats.reasoning: Extended afternoon shift. The transit strike ends at 4pm but Mateo is deep in work mode. Workaholic energy carries him through. Mild weather keeps energy steady compared to yesterday's heat drain.

beats.activity: Bike ride home from Georgetown, transit strike now over
beats.reasoning: Return commute on bike. Metro strike lifted at 4pm but Mateo prefers cycling anyway. ~50 min ride in pleasant evening weather. Arrives home as light softens.
Post-work wind-down. Freshen up after the ride and work day. Mild energy recovery before evening plans.

beats.activity: Bike ride to thrift store in Wallingford, hunting for transit maps and vintage finds
beats.reasoning: Mateo has a thrifting hobby and recently found an 80s transit map at Wallingford—this is a natural evening errand for him. Easy ~3 km ride from Northgate. Thursday evening, store still open.

beats.activity: Browse thrift store, catalog finds, chat with owner about local history
beats.reasoning: Mateo values solitary or low-key social time over crowds. Thrifting is his meditative hobby. He'll spend an hour hunting for transit maps or old Seattle cycling ephemera, then head home.
Return home on bike. ~3 km ride, ~20 min. Arrives as evening cools down. Perfect timing for settling in for the night.

beats.activity: Evening at home: tend rooftop garden, catalog thrift finds, beer and a book
beats.reasoning: Evening ritual. Water the garden, deadhead spent flowers, organize any transit maps or vintage finds from thrift run. Relax with a beer and read about Seattle bike history or routes. Low-key wind-down matching his mood.

7 hours 45 minutes of solid sleep. Ends before 06:30 the next morning. Mateo will wake refreshed and ready for Friday work after a moderate-energy day balancing work, exercise (biking), and leisure hobbies.

world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
